name: Prompt Sensitivity And Cross Run Suite
description: Propose a benchmark concept for prompt sensitivity and cross run suite.
related_rules: Prompt sensitivity and cross-run stability MUST be measured explicitly for all judge and scoring subsystems.
Template echo detection MUST run on any output produced under few-shot or example-based prompting regimes.
related_validators: Apply template echo detector to sample batches for each major prompt or fine-tune version, adjusting thresholds using benchmark fixtures.
For any change to prompts, rubrics, or fixtures, record a new version identifier and run prompt sensitivity and stability checks.
Use regression tests to block deployments where stability improves but agreement with human or reference metrics degrades.
related_failure_modes: LLM-as-judge exhibits prompt sensitivity, self-preference, and rubric drift, masking true quality.
Stability metrics report "stable but wrong" patterns where outputs are consistent yet misaligned with references or human judgment.
